item.title: Bessent Says Trump’s Tariff Rates Could Be Restored by July
item.description: President Donald Trump’s tariffs may be restored by July to the levels in place before the Supreme Court struck down many of his levies, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said.

item.title: Anthropic Wins Accolades From Canada’s AI Minister Over Mythos Approach
item.description: A Canadian cabinet minister praised Anthropic PBC’s decision to introduce its Mythos model to select companies and allow them to test the technology before releasing it more widely.

item.title: SEC Approves Plan Removing Day-Trading Limit for Small Investors
item.description: The US Securities and Exchange Commission gave the go-ahead for sweeping changes to a restriction on day-trading activity by small investors on Tuesday in a move cheered by retail brokers.

item.title: AI Chatbots Give Misleading Medical Advice 50% of the Time, Study Finds
item.description: Artificial intelligence-driven chatbots are giving users problematic medical advice about half the time, according to a new study, highlighting the health risks of the technology that’s becoming increasingly integral in day-to-day life.

item.title: Gold Holds Gain as Renewed Push for US-Iran Talks Eases Risks
item.description: Gold held a gain on optimism that the US and Iran are seeking a negotiated settlement to the war, easing inflation concerns arising from an energy-supply shock.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Track US Rally on Iran Peace Push: Markets Wrap
item.description: Stocks in Asia are set to open higher after the S&P 500 rallied to near a fresh record, as optimism around further peace talks between the US and Iran pushed down oil prices.

item.title: Amazon-Globalstar Deal Is Poised to Boost Apple’s Satellite Ambitions
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s takeover of Globalstar Inc. is poised to benefit one of the company’s biggest rivals in hardware devices: Apple Inc.

item.title: Oil Steadies as Traders Track Moves to Restart Iran-US Talks
item.description: Oil held a drop, with the US and Iran looking to arrange a second round of peace talks in the coming days, as a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz continued to impede energy supplies vital for the world economy.

item.title: Daiichi Life to Tighten Private Credit Manager Selection on Risk
item.description: Daiichi Life Group Inc. is tightening the process for selecting managers of private credit investments to decrease risk after several high-profile defaults overseas.

item.title: Hormuz Closure Sets Clock Ticking for Asia’s Rice Farmers
item.description: The war in Iran is threatening Asia’s rice harvest. Today on the Big Take Asia: What this means for the region’s tables, economies and politics.

item.title: Meta, Broadcom Deepen Ties on Chips; Tan Departs Meta Board
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. announced an expanded multibillion-dollar partnership with Broadcom Inc. to design and build custom chips to power the social media giant’s artificial intelligence efforts.

item.title: Indonesia Finance Chief Takes His Growth Bet to a World on Edge
item.description: Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa’s bluntness often jars. Some market watchers were taken aback in February when the Indonesian finance minster suggested a Citibank Inc. analyst with two master’s degrees wasn’t a true economist because he lacked a PhD. Last week, he called the World Bank’s 4.7% growth projection for this year a “major mistake.”

item.title: Read the Edited Highlights of Purbaya’s Interview With Bloomberg
item.description: Indonesia’s Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa sat down for a wide ranging, exclusive interview with Bloomberg News. Here are the edited highlights of the conversation.

item.title: Traders Target Bond Market Rally on Iran War ‘Tone Change’
item.description: Optimism about a possible peace deal in the Middle East and lower oil prices has bond traders positioning for gains in Treasuries that could send 10-year yields sliding toward 4%.

item.title: Wall Street Investors Learn to Live With War as Stocks Surge
item.description: It’s been two months since the war in Iran began, with no clear end in sight. But after some initial turbulence, Wall Street seems to be blocking out the noise.

item.title: Amazon’s $11.6 Billion Globalstar Deal Amps Up Rivalry With Musk
item.description: Amazon.com Inc.’s $11.6 billion deal to buy Globalstar Inc. ramps up competition with Elon Musk’s Starlink in satellite communications, a market predicted to double to $200 billion in the coming years.

item.title: Lumen CEO Says AI Bots Are Taking Over the Internet
item.description: Over half of the planet’s internet traffic is now made up of AI bots, according to Kate Johnson, chief executive officer of enterprise network giant Lumen Technologies Inc., forcing executives across sectors to rethink how their companies handle everything from customer-service requests to hidden network threats.

item.title: OpenAI Releases Cyber Model to Limited Group in Race With Mythos
item.description: OpenAI is letting a select group of users access a new artificial intelligence model that’s meant to be more adept at spotting software security vulnerabilities, one week after rival Anthropic PBC announced a limited release of an AI tool called Mythos.

item.title: US Eases Venezuela Sanctions to Allow Central Bank Transactions
item.description: The US is easing sanctions on Venezuela to allow parties to conduct transactions with the country’s central bank as the Trump administration pushes to revive the nation’s oil sector following the capture of former president Nicolás Maduro.

item.title: IMF, Lebanon Hold Talks on Up to $1 Billion in Rapid Financing
item.description: The International Monetary Fund and Lebanon are discussing options for providing fast-track assistance to help the country absorb the impact of the Middle East war, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: A Tax Day Cash Drain Is Coming for Calm US Funding Markets
item.description: Wall Street strategists expect a robust increase in the Treasury’s coffers this week as Americans pay their taxes, potentially putting pressure on relatively calm US funding markets.

item.title: Argentina Inflation Picked Up More Than Expected on Energy Shock
item.description: Argentina’s inflation picked up more than expected in March as prices at the pump rose sharply on the Iran war and as education costs surged on annual tuition adjustments.

item.title: Kenya Hikes Gasoline Pump Prices Sharply on Iran War
item.description: Kenya pump prices jumped to the highest in nearly three years as the fallout from the war on Iran reverberates across economies.

item.title: Bond Market Veteran Bassman Sues ETF Manager Over Compensation
item.description: Harley Bassman, who invented a widely-followed gauge of Treasury-market volatility and later developed exchange-traded funds for Simplify Asset Management, is suing the firm for allegedly stiffing him of millions of dollars in compensation.

item.title: Iran Offers Limited Internet in Rare Move to Stem War Losses
item.description: Iran has started restoring limited internet connectivity to some businesses and individuals, a sign it’s seeking to mitigate the economic damage from the nationwide shutdown that started with the outbreak of war with the US and Israel more than six weeks ago.

item.title: Fintech SumUp Lines Up Banks for Possible London IPO
item.description: SumUp Payments Ltd. is lining up investment banks to arrange a potential initial public offering in London, according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be one of the biggest listings the UK capital has seen in recent years.

item.title: Undisclosed Missed Debt Payment Spurs Superdowngrade in Illinois
item.description: A roughly 120-year-old park district in central Illinois that failed to disclose a missed municipal-bond payment for several months had its credit rating slashed five levels into junk by S&P Global Ratings, which signaled another downgrade is possible.

item.title: Tory Burch Seeks Loan to Buy Back General Atlantic’s Stake
item.description: Luxury retailer Tory Burch LLC intends to repurchase private equity firm General Atlantic’s stake with some of the proceeds of a planned $700 million leveraged loan.

item.title: BOE’s Bailey Urges Regulators to Assess AI Cyber Risk to Banks
item.description: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said global regulators need to rapidly evaluate the threat posed by Anthropic’s Mythos artificial intelligence model.

item.title: Canada Regulator Reviewing Banks’ Private-Credit Exposures
item.description: Canada’s bank regulator is examining lenders’ exposure to hedge funds and private-credit shops — as well as how they transfer risk to those firms — with further inquiries planned.
